acceptance_targets: [CH15_CRAFT_TELEPATHY_HANDOUT] Pass if the revised opening at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:17-31 makes the assassination setup legible only after Anya combines at least two partial thoughts with visible room geometry, and if the revised relay beat at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:395-419 either shows why 'behind the booster' follows from earlier text or depicts Anya and Loid searching for the device. Fail if one clean surface thought or an unexplained leap still hands her the answer whole.

[CH15_CRAFT_RELAY_TIMING_BREAK] Pass if a cold reader can compute from snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:197-233, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:259-313, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:391-435 when the relay activates and why the number at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:431 is possible. Fail if the reader still has to invent off-page minutes or reinterpret the timer after the fact to make the arithmetic work.

[CH15_CRAFT_SCREENPLAY_DRIFT] Pass if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:101-123 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:235-257 no longer contain any 10-plus-paragraph run without Damian's or Loid's thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment, and if each exchange gains at least two interior beats that alter the scene's pressure. Fail if those passages still read like a transcript with names removed.

[CH15_CRAFT_SOFTENED_FALLOUT_CLOSE] Pass if the revised ending at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:463-473 leaves Loid's knowledge of Anya's lie and Crane's death materially tense on the page, with a specific sign of fracture that coexists with any tenderness. Fail if the dominant takeaway of the final exchange is reassurance or provisional absolution rather than unresolved cost.
[RED-011] Ch15's opening paragraph does not restate any of the three phrases from Ch14 L4055-4057 (separate entrance, sight lines, list he'd kept her off).
[length_chapter_15_min_words] Chapter word count is >= 7000 while preserving heading contract.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised opening at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:17-31 makes the assassination setup legible only after Anya combines at least two partial thoughts with visible room geometry, and if the revised relay beat at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:395-419 either shows why 'behind the booster' follows from earlier text or depicts Anya and Loid searching for the device. Fail if one clean surface thought or an unexplained leap still hands her the answer whole.
findings.problem: The chapter repeatedly lets Anya's telepathy function as reader-ready plot delivery instead of noisy surface reading. The assassination plot arrives through unusually complete operative thoughts, and the relay beat then jumps from partial clues to Anya giving an exact physical instruction ('Behind the booster') that the page has not earned. That drifts into telepathy-as-omniscience and weakens the summit's investigative pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the clue chain at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:17-31, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:45-61, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:395-419 so the critical thoughts stay fragmentary, coded, or incomplete, and Anya has to combine them with what she can physically observe. If the relay remains hidden behind the booster, either seed that exact physical detail earlier in the same chapter or stage a short on-page search once she reaches the room.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader can compute from snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:197-233, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:259-313, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:391-435 when the relay activates and why the number at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:431 is possible. Fail if the reader still has to invent off-page minutes or reinterpret the timer after the fact to make the arithmetic work.
findings.problem: The relay clock does not match the chapter's own timestamps. Loid finds the note at 13:42, Falk says the terrace team is eight minutes out, and Loid kills the main board before Damian's floor speech and the south-terrace fight. By 14:07, the hidden relay cannot still be at 'Eleven minutes, forty seconds' and 'Almost the full buffer' unless the timer means something other than the text currently implies. The beat asks the reader to track exact clock logic, then breaks it.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the chronology across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:197-233, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:259-313, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:391-435 so the relay duration, the 14:07 timestamp, and the main-board shutdown all describe one coherent sequence. Options are fine, but the text must explicitly support one of them: move when Loid kills the board, change the 14:07 stamp, change the 11:40 reading, or clarify that the status screen is measuring something other than elapsed relay uptime.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:101-123 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:235-257 no longer contain any 10-plus-paragraph run without Damian's or Loid's thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment, and if each exchange gains at least two interior beats that alter the scene's pressure. Fail if those passages still read like a transcript with names removed.
findings.problem: Two of the chapter's biggest exchanges drop close-third mediation for 10+ consecutive dialogue paragraphs. The Damian/Voss stretch from line 101 to line 121 and the Loid/Falk stretch from line 235 to line 257 largely run as transcript, so the focalizers disappear right when the chapter most needs their embodied processing. The result is screenplay drift.
findings.rewrite_direction: Thread focal thought, sensation, or self-assessment back into snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:101-123 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:235-257. The new interior beats should not summarize the argument; they should show Damian and Loid registering leverage shifts, bodily stress, or interpretive surprise in ways that change how the next spoken line lands.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised ending at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:463-473 leaves Loid's knowledge of Anya's lie and Crane's death materially tense on the page, with a specific sign of fracture that coexists with any tenderness. Fail if the dominant takeaway of the final exchange is reassurance or provisional absolution rather than unresolved cost.
findings.problem: The chapter lands the devastating fact that Loid now understands Anya's false report helped Falk exploit the blind channels, then immediately cushions that rupture with an affirming line and physical comfort. 'You did good work today' plus the shoulder touch reads like a moral and emotional safety valve at the exact point where the chapter should let the damage stay live. That is sanitization drift, not earned restraint.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the closing movement at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:447-473 so Loid can defer the full conversation without functionally absolving Anya in the same beat. Warmth can remain, but it needs a counterweight: visible recoil, an unfinished question, an operational instruction, a failed attempt at comfort, or some other concrete sign that the father-handler breach is still open.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch15's opening paragraph does not restate any of the three phrases from Ch14 L4055-4057 (separate entrance, sight lines, list he'd kept her off).
findings.evidence: Ch14 L4055: 'East Gallery meant a separate entrance; better sight lines'; Ch14 L4057: 'Tomorrow she'd walk into the conference center wearing his press badge and still not know what list he'd kept her off'; Ch15 L4062: 'The borrowed badge had given her better sight lines; a separate entrance; and still no answer about what list he'd kept her off.'; Chapter 14 closes by telling the reader Anya still does not know 'what list he'd kept her off; ' and chapter 15 opens by repeating that the borrowed badge still leaves her with 'no answer about what list he'd kept her off.' snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4057; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4062

evidence.excerpt: 4057: Tomorrow she'd walk into the conference center wearing his press badge and still not know what list he'd kept her off.

4062: The International Conference Center smelled like floor polish and new carpet, the kind of institutional freshness that meant someone important was expected. Anya took her seat in the East Gallery, Damian's borrowed press credential clipped to her lapel, and opened herself to the room. The borrowed badge had given her better sight lines, a separate entrance, and still no answer about what list he'd kept her off.

findings.problem: Ch15's opening sentence recaps three specific phrases from Ch14's closing in substantially the same form: 'separate entrance, better sight lines' (reversed word order), 'press badge' / 'borrowed badge' (near-synonym), and 'still not know what list he'd kept her off' / 'still no answer about what list he'd kept her off' (12 of 13 content words identical). This is a classic chapter-seam recap that treats the chapter break as a memory gap the reader needs help crossing.
findings.rewrite_direction: Cut the Chapter 14 wording echo from Chapter 15's opening paragraph and replace it, if needed, with a fresh consequence of Damian's badge reroute or of Anya's summit position instead of repeating "better sight lines / separate entrance / what list he'd kept her off."

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 7000 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's premise blends comedy, espionage action, and genuine emotional stakes. The creative risk is in honoring all three simultaneously without letting any one mode cannibalize the others. Do not soften the violence because the novel is funny. Do not suppress the comedy because the stakes are real. Do not retreat from Anya's moral complicity (she reads minds without consent and uses it to manipulate) because she is the protagonist and we like her. The hardest scenes to get right are the ones where comedy and pain coexist — a fight that is also funny, a confession that is also absurd, a family dinner where love and deception are the same meal. Lean into those moments rather than resolving them into one register. The novel's best material lives in the dissonance.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When violence, fear, grief, or moral compromise demands scene presence, render it with specificity. A bruise should have a color. A death should have weight. A lie should have a cost the liar feels in their body. The exception is Anya's telepathic restraint — she sometimes cannot bear to fully articulate what she's reading in someone's mind, and this selective omission is a character choice, not authorial squeamishness.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in casual speech. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific register choices (Sylvia's professional diction, Damian's diplomatic mode). In contemporary casual scenes, an unjustified full-form line should be flagged and checked.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should share the same structural rhythm. Alternate between dialogue-heavy scenes and interiority-heavy passages. Vary scene count: two-scene chapters (11, 16, 17 — compressed force) against multi-scene chapters (8, 15 — breadth and momentum). Shift between comic dominant and dramatic dominant across the middle of the book, with the comic register maintaining presence even in dramatic chapters through Anya's voice and the Forger family's inherent absurdity. The tonal shift at Chapter 13 is a deliberate gear change, not a gradual dimming — comedy should be fully alive in Chapter 12 and markedly reduced (not absent) in Chapter 13. The Chapter 13 POV shift to Damian is a structural event: the reader's relationship to information changes. The final act (14-17) maintains Anya's voice and the family's warmth as comic undertone, but the dominant mode is drama with consequences. Chapter 17's dinner scene reintroduces comedy through a register the novel has not previously accessed — Damian's self-conscious thought management around a known telepath — which only works because it follows sixteen chapters of oblivious thought-reading.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary and accessible, not literary-precious. The prose should have energy and forward drive. Humor comes from observation, character logic, and the gap between surface and interior — not from clever narration or authorial commentary. Let the funny things be funny without signposting them. Let the sad things sit without interpretation. The prose should be invisible when it needs to be and vivid when the scene demands it, but never decorative for its own sake.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Narrator-as-comedian: the prose commenting on its own comedy, winking at the reader, or admiring a character's wit. The humor lives in the situation, not in the telling.

Telepathy-as-omniscience: Anya's mind-reading should feel like cognitive overload, not like a narrator's convenient access to exposition. She reads surface thoughts, not deep motivations, and the thoughts she catches should be fragmentary, mundane, and sometimes misleading.

Prestige-creep: the prose drifting toward solemn literary register when the scene's natural mode is comic, warm, or chaotic. This novel's emotional power comes from the coexistence of comedy and genuine feeling, not from the comedy yielding to seriousness.

Briefing-itis: scenes where characters explain plot to each other or to the reader. Information should emerge through action, observation, and telepathic fragments — not through expository dialogue formatted as conversation.

Combat-poetry: fight scenes that become lyrical or aesthetically admiring. Violence should be fast, specific, physically grounded, and over quickly. Yor's lethality is matter-of-fact; Anya's fighting is scrappier and less elegant.

Emotion-labeling: telling the reader what a character feels instead of rendering the feeling through thought, action, and physical detail. 'She felt sad' is always worse than the specific thing that makes the sadness legible.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, primarily Anya. Chapter 7 (Damian), Chapter 10 (Yor), the Damian POV section in Chapter 13, and Damian's thread in Chapter 15 shift to close third on their respective POV characters. The telepathy layer is unique to Anya's POV — when she is the viewpoint character, other characters' surface thoughts appear as italicized interjections woven into the prose. In non-Anya POV chapters, no telepathic content appears. The Chapter 13 Damian POV section is structurally critical: it gives the reader access to Damian's interior at a depth Anya's telepathy cannot reach, and it reveals that he has identified Anya — knowledge the reader carries into the final act while Anya does not. This dramatic irony reversal is the novel's strongest structural surprise and depends on the reader experiencing Damian's depths unmediated by Anya's telepathic filter.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and responsive to scene pressure. Action sequences: short sentences, concrete verbs, sensory immediacy. Comic scenes: longer sentences that build to turns, pacing that allows beats to land. Quiet domestic scenes: relaxed rhythm with room for observation and texture. Telepathic interjections should disrupt prose rhythm the way intrusive thoughts disrupt concentration — not ornamentally but as genuine cognitive interference. Avoid metronomic sentence patterns in any mode.
sensory_bias: visual surveillance — reading rooms, reading faces, the spy's hyperawareness of spatial arrangement and exit routes
auditory — overheard thoughts as a telepathic sixth sense layered over actual ambient sound
kinesthetic — action choreography, physical proximity, the body under stress or in intimacy
olfactory/gustatory — Yor's cooking, Berlint street food, the specific smell of institutional buildings and safe houses
